title: Typical entanglement entropy with charge conservation
authors: Eugenio Bianchi, Pietro Donà, Erick Muiño
abstract: We consider a many-body Hilbert space with a fixed global charge and show that the typical entanglement entropy of a subsystem, at the leading and subleading order in the thermodynamic limit, can be expressed in terms of a single quantity which represents the local thermal entropy at fixed charge density. We find a general formula which applies both to abelian U(1) symmetry and non-abelian SU(2) symmetry, including the case of a local Hilbert space which transforms under a general reducible representation of the symmetry group. We illustrate the general formula with model systems and discuss the relevance of the results as a probe of quantum chaos for physical Hamiltonians.
summary: This paper provides a general framework for calculating the typical entanglement entropy in many-body systems with fixed global charges. By mapping the problem to a thermodynamic context, the authors show that the entanglement entropy scales according to a local thermal entropy function. The results cover both abelian U(1) and non-abelian SU(2) symmetries, demonstrating that the entropy is highly concentrated around its average. This work offers a powerful tool for studying the entanglement properties of quantum chaotic systems and the effects of symmetry on information scrambling.
main_problem: Determining the asymptotic behavior and typicality of entanglement entropy in many-body systems subject to fixed global abelian (U(1)) and non-abelian (SU(2)) charge conservation.
main_result: The authors derive a general formula for the average entanglement entropy that can be expressed via a single quantity representing local thermal entropy, and show that the variance is exponentially suppressed, making the entropy 'typical'.
method: The study employs asymptotic analysis using Laplace's method and the saddle-point approximation, alongside group theory (character theory and Haar measures) and information theory (Kullback-Leibler divergence).
model_or_system: The framework applies to any many-body system with a local single-charge symmetry group in the thermodynamic limit, including specific models like qubits, qutrits, and spin-trimers under U(1) and SU(2) symmetries.
key_observables: Average entanglement entropy, variance of entanglement entropy, and local entropy/heat capacity.
important_parameters: Thermodynamic limit (N approaching infinity), subsystem fraction (f), global charge density (s), and local Hilbert space dimension (k).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes a finite-dimensional local Hilbert space and focuses on the thermodynamic limit; it also assumes the smoothness of the integrand except in specific critical regimes.
figures_summary: Figure 1 plots local entropy versus charge density for various U(1) models; Figure 2 is mentioned but its specific content is not detailed in the notes.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of charge-conserved entanglement, develops a thermodynamic mapping using relative entropy, provides asymptotic expansions for U(1) and SU(2) symmetries, analyzes specific model examples, and discusses the implications for quantum chaos and typicality.
why_interesting: This is highly relevant for many-body dynamics as it provides a universal thermodynamic interpretation of entanglement scaling and establishes how global symmetries influence the entanglement properties of random states, which is crucial for understanding thermalization and quantum chaos.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: The local entropy η(s) for the case of: (left) N qubits with fixed global U(1) charge, (center) N qutrits with fixed global U(1) charge, and (right) N two-species hardcore bosons with fixed global U(1) charge corresponding to fixed total number of particles.
